title: config
description: Configuration options for Doc Detective operations.

input.description: Path(s) to test specifications and documentation source files. May be paths to specific files or to directories to scan for files.

output.description: Path of the directory in which to store the output of Doc Detective commands. If a file path is specified, Doc Detective attempts to honor the file name specified, but file path behavior is controlled by the configured reporters.

reporters.description: Reporters to use when emitting test results. Built-in reporters: `terminal`, `json`, `html`, `runFolder`. The `runFolder` reporter (on by default) archives each run's results as `<output>/.doc-detective/runs/<runId>/testResults.json` (or `coverageResults.json` for coverage runs), beside any screenshots the run captured, in addition to the flat output the `json` reporter writes. You can also reference custom reporters registered via `registerReporter()` by name.

specFilter.description: Regex patterns (case-insensitive) applied to each spec's `specId`. If set, only specs whose `specId` matches at least one pattern are run. Equivalent to `--spec` on the CLI. Each entry must contain at least one non-whitespace character.

testFilter.description: Regex patterns (case-insensitive) applied to each test's `testId`. If set, only tests whose `testId` matches at least one pattern are run. Equivalent to `--test` on the CLI. Each entry must contain at least one non-whitespace character.

recursive.description: If `true` searches `input`, `setup`, and `cleanup` paths recursively for test specifications and source files.

relativePathBase.description: Whether paths should be interpreted as relative to the current working directory (`cwd`) or to the file in which they're specified (`file`).

origin.description: Default protocol and domain to use for relative URLs.

originParams.description: Query parameters to append to URLs resolved against `origin`. Values support environment variable substitution via `$VAR` syntax. Step-level `params` on `goTo` / `checkLink` are merged on top of these, with step keys winning on collision. WARNING: values are embedded in request URLs and appear verbatim in test results, logs, and reports — avoid putting long-lived secrets here.

beforeAny.description: Path(s) to test specifications to perform before those specified by `input`. Useful for setting up testing environments.

afterAll.description: Path(s) to test specifications to perform after those specified by `input`. Useful for cleaning up testing environments.

detectSteps.description: Whether or not to detect steps in input files based on defined markup.

allowUnsafeSteps.description: Whether or not to run potentially unsafe steps, such as those that might modify files or system state.

crawl.description: If `true`, crawls sitemap.xml files specified by URL to find additional files to test.

processDitaMaps.description: If `true`, processes DITA maps and includes generated files as inputs.

logLevel.description: Amount of detail to output when performing an operation.

fileTypes.description: Configuration for file types and their markup detection.

anyOf.$comment: Simple mode: Reference predefined templates by name.
anyOf.title: File type (predefined)

anyOf.$comment: Custom mode: Extend predefined templates or write whole new ones.
anyOf.title: File type (custom)

extensions.description: File extensions to use with type.

markup.description: Markup definitions for the file type.

anyOf.title: File type (executable)
anyOf.$comment: Executable mode: Convert executable inputs directly into tests. additionalProperties is false so this branch can't silently absorb malformed custom file types.

extensions.description: File extensions to use with type.

runShell.description: `runShell` step to perform for this file type. Use $1 as a placeholder for the file path.

integrations.description: Options for connecting to external services.

docDetectiveApi.description: Configuration for Doc Detective Orchestration API integration.

apiKey.description: API key for authenticating with the Doc Detective Orchestration API.

docDetectiveApi.title: Doc Detective Orchestration API

heretto.description: Configuration for Heretto CMS integrations. Each entry specifies a Heretto instance and a scenario to build and test.

name.description: Unique identifier for this Heretto integration. Used in logs and results.

organizationId.description: The organization subdomain used to access Heretto CCMS (e.g., 'thunderbird' for thunderbird.heretto.com).

username.description: Heretto CCMS username (email address) for API authentication.

apiToken.description: API token generated in Heretto CCMS for authentication. See https://help.heretto.com/en/heretto-ccms/api/ccms-api-authentication/basic-authentication#ariaid-title3

scenarioName.description: Name of the scenario to build and test.

outputPath.description: Local path where Heretto content was downloaded. Set automatically during processing.

fileMapping.description: Mapping of local file paths to Heretto file metadata. Set automatically during content loading.

fileId.description: The UUID of the file in Heretto.

filePath.description: The path of the file in Heretto.

uploadOnChange.description: If `true`, uploads changed screenshots and other media files back to Heretto CMS after test execution.

resourceDependencies.description: Mapping of Heretto file paths to their UUIDs and metadata. Set automatically during content loading by fetching ditamap resource dependencies.

uuid.description: The UUID of the file in Heretto.

fullPath.description: The full xmldb path of the file in Heretto.

parentFolderId.description: The UUID of the parent folder in Heretto.

heretto.title: Heretto CMS integrations

integrations.title: Integrations options

telemetry.description: Options around sending telemetry for Doc Detective usage.

userId.description: Identifier for the organization, group, or individual running Doc Detective.

telemetry.title: Telemetry options

hints.description: Options for the post-run hints feature. After a test run, Doc Detective may print one short, contextual hint with code samples and links to encourage further engagement (for example, suggesting a CI workflow when none is detected). Doc Detective displays hints only in an interactive terminal (TTY) and only at the default `info` log level.

enabled.description: If `true` (the default), Doc Detective may print one applicable hint after a test run. Disable from the CLI with `--no-hints`.

hints.title: Hints options

concurrentRunners.description: Number of concurrent test runners. Set to true to use CPU core count (capped at 4).

debug.description: Deprecated and ignored. Previously reserved for an interactive step-through debugger that was never implemented. Retained so existing configs continue to validate. For diagnostics, run `doc-detective debug` or set the `DOC_DETECTIVE_DEBUG` environment variable.

dryRun.description: If `true`, fully resolve tests (file/env config merge, schema validation, file detection, inline-test extraction) and emit the resolved test plan as JSON, but do not execute any steps. Equivalent to `--dry-run` on the CLI.

autoScreenshot.description: If `true`, captures a screenshot after every browser-driven step that does not already define an explicit `screenshot`. Steps with an explicit `screenshot` are skipped for auto-capture since they already produce an image. Doc Detective saves images in the per-run artifact directory (`<output>/.doc-detective/runs/<runId>/`) under the nested resource tree, with each image named by the step's order, action, and ID (for example, `specs/<specId>/tests/<testId>/contexts/<contextId>/screenshots/01-goTo-s4f2a91c.png`), so the same step lands on the same relative path in every run's folder for run-over-run comparison. Specs and tests can override this value with their own `autoScreenshot` fields (test level wins over spec level, which wins over config level). Equivalent to `--auto-screenshot` on the CLI.

annotationDefaults.description: Default visual theme for screenshot and recording annotations. Specs and tests can override this value with their own `annotationDefaults` fields (test level wins over spec level, which wins over config level), and an individual annotation's `style` wins over all of them. Fields left unset fall back to Doc Detective's built-in theme. Setting a theme here keeps every annotated image in a doc set visually consistent.

autoRecord.description: If `true`, records a video of every test context that runs in a browser, in addition to any explicit `record` steps. The recording wraps the whole context (it starts before the first step and stops after the last) and always uses the `ffmpeg` engine. Videos are saved in the per-run artifact directory (`<output>/.doc-detective/runs/<runId>/`) under the nested resource tree (for example, `specs/<specId>/tests/<testId>/contexts/<contextId>/recordings/<contextId>.mp4`), so the same context lands on the same relative path in every run's folder for run-over-run comparison. Specs and tests can override this value with their own `autoRecord` fields (test level wins over spec level, which wins over config level). Equivalent to `--auto-record` on the CLI.

autoUpdate.description: If `true` (default), the CLI checks for a newer published `doc-detective` on startup and self-updates before running tests. Updates happen for global (`npm i -g`) and `npx` installs only — local installs (where `doc-detective` is a project dep) get an informational message instead, since auto-updating would mutate the user's lockfile. CI environments and the `DOC_DETECTIVE_SKIP_AUTO_UPDATE=1` env var also skip the check. Set to `false` to pin to the installed version. Equivalent to `--no-auto-update` on the CLI.

shell.description: Default shell for `runShell` steps (and `runCode`'s shell-based execution). `runShell` steps can override this value with their own `shell` field. `cmd` and `powershell` are only supported on Windows. On Windows, `bash` resolves to Git Bash, which Doc Detective installs automatically if it isn't present.

cacheDir.description: Directory for lazy-installed runtime assets (heavy npm packages, browser binaries, ffmpeg). Defaults to `<os.tmpdir()>/doc-detective/`. Override here, with the `DOC_DETECTIVE_CACHE_DIR` env var, or with `--cache-dir` on the CLI when the default temp location is unsuitable (e.g., baked container images where temp gets cleared on reboot).

browserFallback.description: Controls whether a context whose browser cannot start a driver session falls back to another available browser instead of being skipped. Drivers are validated by execution (not just presence) so a present-but-broken driver — for example a partially downloaded geckodriver — no longer silently skips Firefox coverage. `auto` (default): fall back to any other available browser for both auto-selected and explicitly requested browsers; a fallback away from an explicitly requested browser reports the context as `WARNING` rather than `PASS`. `explicit`: fall back only when the browser was auto-selected; an explicitly requested browser whose driver is broken is skipped with a diagnostic reason. `off`: never fall back across browsers (driver validation and diagnostic skip reasons still apply). Equivalent to `--browser-fallback` on the CLI.

environment.description: Environment information for the system running Doc Detective.

workingDirectory.description: The current working directory of the process running Doc Detective.

platform.description: The operating system type running Doc Detective.

arch.description: The processor architecture of the system running Doc Detective.

environment.title: Environment details

anyOf.$comment: Regex mode: match raw file text with regular expressions.
anyOf.title: Markup definition (regex)

regex.description: Regular expressions to match the markup type.

batchMatches.description: If `true`, all matches are combined into a single string.

markupActions.description: Actions to perform when the markup type is detected.

markupSelectorDefinition.$comment: Selector mode: match structurally parsed semantic nodes. Exactly one selector kind key (comment, codeBlock, link, image, strong, emphasis, text, element) per definition, mirroring the action-as-key pattern of the step schema.
markupSelectorDefinition.title: Markup definition (selector)

codeBlock.description: Match fenced/source code blocks. A string is shorthand for `{ "language": "<value>" }`.

text.description: Match prose text runs (never inside code blocks or comments).

element.description: Match a named element (HTML/XML tag or MDX JSX component). A string is shorthand for `{ "tag": "<value>" }`.

captures.description: Node field paths mapped in order to $1..$n for action substitution. Supported paths per kind: `content`, `text`, `language`, `meta`, `url`, `title`, `src`, `alt`, `tag`, `attributes.<name>`, `match.<n>`, and `then.<field>`. If omitted, the kind's documented default capture order applies.

batchMatches.description: If `true`, all matches are combined into a single string.

matches.description: Regular expression the node's content must match. Capture groups are exposed as `match.<n>` capture paths.

excludes.description: Regular expression the node's content must not match.

selectorAttributes.title: Attribute matchers
selectorAttributes.description: Map of attribute name to matcher: an exact/regex string, or `true` to require that the attribute exists. For Markdown/MDX, attributes come from Kramdown/Pandoc-style attribute lists (`{: .cls #id key="val"}`): classes normalize to `class`, IDs to `id`.

language.description: Info-string language(s) to match. An empty string matches bare fences with no language.

metaMatches.description: Regular expression the info-string tail (after the language) must match.

metaExcludes.description: Regular expression the info-string tail must not match.

contentMatches.description: Regular expression the block content must match. Capture groups are exposed as `match.<n>` capture paths.

contentExcludes.description: Regular expression the block content must not match.

url.description: Regular expression the link destination must match.

text.description: Regular expression the link text must match.

src.description: Regular expression the image source must match.

alt.description: Regular expression the image alt text must match.

text.description: Regular expression the node's text must match.

contentMatches.description: Regular expression the element's inner content must match. Capture groups are exposed as `match.<n>` capture paths.

contentExcludes.description: Regular expression the element's inner content must not match.

selectorPrecededBy.title: Preceding-context matcher
selectorPrecededBy.description: Regular expression matched against the sibling text immediately before the node, for verb-context patterns (e.g. "Click **X**").

selectorFollowedBy.title: Following-context matcher
selectorFollowedBy.description: Regular expression matched against the sibling text immediately after the node, or an object chaining a second node for sequence patterns. Chained-node fields are exposed as `then.<field>` capture paths.

text.description: Regular expression the sibling text between this node and the chained node must match.

selectorNode.$comment: A bare selector (exactly one kind key, no captures/actions). Used for followedBy.then chaining and statement containers. Kind options here exclude precededBy/followedBy to keep chains single-level.
selectorNode.title: Selector node

statementContainer.title: Statement container
statementContainer.description: Where inline statements live: the string "comment" (all comment nodes), or a selector node whose matched nodes are read as statements. `value` names the node field holding the statement text (defaults to the node's own content).

value.description: Field path holding the statement text, e.g. `attributes.value`. Defaults to the node's own content.

inlineStatements.description: Statements to include tests and steps inside the content of the file, such as within Markdown.

in.description: Statement containers for structurally parsed file types: which semantic nodes are read as potential inline statements. Coexists with the regex statement properties below.

testStart.description: Regular expressions that indicate the start of a test. If capture groups are used, the first capture group is used for the statement. If no capture groups are used, the entire match is used for the statement.

testEnd.description: Regular expressions that indicate that the current test is complete.

ignoreStart.description: Regular expressions that indicates that the following content should be ignored for testing purposes.

ignoreEnd.description: Regular expressions that indicate that the ignored section of content is complete.

step.description: Regular expressions that indicate a step in a test.

inlineStatements.title: Inline statement definition
